block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: At the close of trading on 24 August, Tesla’s 3-for-1 split went into effect. It means the number of Tesla shares in circulation increased threefold, which has cut the price of each share by two-thirds, from $891.30 to $297.10. The move is designed to make Tesla stock more accessible to smaller investors, which could broaden the company's shareholder base.
block_07: The company revealed its superconducting quantum computer debut dubbed "Qianshi," with a 10-quantum-bit (qubit) processor at Quantum Create 2022 – the share price rose by 16% in one week.
block_10: The share price has fallen by 21% this week after Piper Sandler’s analysts warned that there could be a backlog of inventory for its plant-based jerky product.
